title: First pregnancy: nutrition and prenatal visit prep
excerpt: Early folic acid, prenatal registration, gentle activity, and when to call your clinician — aligned with Vinmec and ACOG guidance.
content:
## Mindset and early steps

A first pregnancy brings joy and worry. Reliable information helps you prepare — but medical decisions stay with your obstetric team.

## Nutrition from the start

- Start **folic acid** before or as soon as you know you are pregnant (as prescribed).
- Eat enough protein, iron, calcium, and fiber; avoid alcohol and smoking.
- Do not take high-dose supplements without advice.

## Prenatal visits and tests

Register early; complete screening tests on schedule (blood type, infections, glucose screening, etc., per local protocol).

## Activity and rest

Light walking and adequate sleep; avoid heavy lifting if restricted.

## Seek care urgently for

Bleeding, severe pain, fever, or inability to keep fluids down.
